evidence.text: Remaining performance obligations increased 17.4%, or $1.02 billion, to $6.92 billion as of June 30, 2022 as compared to $5.90 billion as of December 31, 2021; Total backlog increased 3.0%, or $576.0 million, to $19.85 billion as of June 30, 2022, as compared to $19.27 billion as of December 31, 2021.
evidence.subsection: Current Quarter Financial Results and Significant Operational Trends and Events

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Growing order backlog indicates increased demand and future revenue opportunities.

factors.summary: Input costs are rising, impacting margins.

evidence.text: The decrease in operating margin was primarily due to the normal variability associated with the overall timing of projects and project mix, as well as inefficiencies attributable to supply chain disruptions impacting certain operations and elevated consumables costs.
evidence.subsection: Current Quarter Financial Results and Significant Operational Trends and Events

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Rising input costs pose a risk to profit margins due to supply chain disruptions and elevated expenses.

evidence.text: The decrease in operating margin was primarily due to the normal variability associated with the overall timing of projects and project mix, as well as inefficiencies attributable to supply chain disruptions impacting certain operations and elevated consumables costs.
evidence.subsection: Current Quarter Financial Results and Significant Operational Trends and Events

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Ongoing supply chain disruptions continue to cause inefficiencies and negatively impact operations.

evidence.text: Revenues increased primarily due to increased spending by our utility customers on grid modernization and hardening, as well as higher demand from gas utility and industrial customers.
evidence.subsection: Current Quarter Financial Results and Significant Operational Trends and Events

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Increased construction infrastructure spending suggests expansion and growth opportunities in key sectors.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards larger, more complex projects which yield higher margins but also pose greater risks.

evidence.text: Larger or more complex projects typically include higher voltage transmission projects, pipeline projects, and large-scale renewable generation projects, which yield opportunities for higher margins.
evidence.subsection: Significant Factors Impacting Results

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Shift towards larger, complex projects offers higher margins despite associated risks.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is accelerating due to increased demand in key segments.

evidence.text: Revenues increased due to a $592.2 million increase in revenues from our Renewable Energy Infrastructure Solutions segment, a $383.7 million increase in revenues from our Electric Power Infrastructure Solutions segment, and a $256.3 million increase in revenues from our Underground Utility and Infrastructure Solutions segment.
evidence.subsection: Three months ended June 30, 2022 compared to the three months ended June 30, 2021

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Accelerating organic growth across multiple segments indicates strong demand and expansion.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are growing, driven by increased demand in key segments.

evidence.text: Revenues increased due to a $592.2 million increase in revenues from our Renewable Energy Infrastructure Solutions segment, a $383.7 million increase in revenues from our Electric Power Infrastructure Solutions segment, and a $256.3 million increase in revenues from our Underground Utility and Infrastructure Solutions segment.
evidence.subsection: Three months ended June 30, 2022 compared to the three months ended June 30, 2021

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Growing unit volumes driven by increased demand suggest strong sales and revenue growth.

factors.summary: Labor costs are increasing due to higher compensation expenses and hiring.

evidence.text: The increase was primarily attributable to a $56.1 million increase in expenses associated with acquired businesses, as well as an increase of $14.4 million in compensation expense, associated with increased salaries, incentive compensation, and non-cash stock compensation expense due primarily to increased operational performance and an increase in employees to support the growth of the business.
evidence.subsection: Three months ended June 30, 2022 compared to the three months ended June 30, 2021

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Rising labor costs due to higher compensation and hiring pressures can strain profit margins.

factors.summary: Productivity is improving due to better utilization of resources and fixed cost absorption.

evidence.text: Gross profit increased due primarily to the increase in revenues in all segments and improved utilization and fixed cost absorption in both our Electric Power Infrastructure Solutions segment and Underground Utility and Infrastructure Solutions segment.
evidence.subsection: Three months ended June 30, 2022 compared to the three months ended June 30, 2021

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Improved productivity due to better resource utilization and cost absorption.

factors.summary: Key sensitivities include weather impacts, economic conditions, and regulatory changes.

evidence.text: The results of our business in a given period can be impacted by adverse weather conditions, severe weather events, natural disasters or other emergencies, which include, among other things, heavy or prolonged snowfall or rainfall, hurricanes, tropical storms, tornadoes, floods, blizzards, extreme temperatures, wildfires, post-wildfire floods and debris flows, pandemics (including the ongoing COVID-19 pandemic), and earthquakes.
evidence.subsection: Significant Factors Impacting Results

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: External factors like weather and economic conditions pose risks.

factors.summary: Debt leverage is increasing due to higher levels of long-term debt.

evidence.text: Our long-term debt increased significantly at the end of 2021 in connection with our acquisition of Blattner.
evidence.subsection: Three months ended June 30, 2022 compared to the three months ended June 30, 2021

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Increased long-term debt presents financial risks.

factors.summary: Demand for services is showing mixed trends, with acceleration in certain sectors such as utility and renewable energy, while overall demand is fluctuating due to economic conditions and regulatory requirements.

evidence.text: Revenues increased 41.1%, or $1.2 billion, to $4.23 billion as compared to consolidated revenues of $3.0 billion for the three months ended June 30, 2021; Revenues increased primarily due to increased spending by our utility customers on grid modernization and hardening, as well as higher demand from gas utility and industrial customers.
evidence.subsection: Current Quarter Financial Results and Significant Operational Trends and Events

evidence.text: Demand for our services can vary based on factors such as the financial condition of our customers, their capital spending, and regulatory requirements.
evidence.subsection: Significant Factors Impacting Results

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Mixed demand signals with both growth and fluctuations.

factors.summary: The end markets of renewable energy and electric power infrastructure are experiencing significant growth, with notable increases in revenues across these segments. However, the renewable energy segment faces challenges, including decreased operating margins due to delays and additional costs.

evidence.text: Electric Power Infrastructure Solutions Segment: Revenues increased by 21.1% to $2.20 billion; Underground Utility and Infrastructure Solutions Segment: Revenues increased by 30.1% to $1.11 billion; Renewable Energy Infrastructure Solutions Segment: Revenues increased by 178.4% to $924.2 million, but operating margin decreased due to delays and additional costs.
evidence.subsection: Current Quarter Financial Results and Significant Operational Trends and Events

evidence.text: Revenues increased due to a $592.2 million increase in revenues from our Renewable Energy Infrastructure Solutions segment and a $383.7 million increase in revenues from our Electric Power Infrastructure Solutions segment.
evidence.subsection: Three months ended June 30, 2022 compared to the three months ended June 30, 2021

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Significant growth tempered by margin challenges.

factors.summary: The margin trajectory appears to be mixed, with evidence of both compression and expansion. While gross margins are expanding due to higher revenues and improved utilization, operating margins are compressing, as indicated by their decreasing percentages across multiple segments.

evidence.text: Gross profit increased due primarily to the increase in revenues in all segments and improved utilization and fixed cost absorption in both our Electric Power Infrastructure Solutions segment and Underground Utility and Infrastructure Solutions segment.
evidence.subsection: Current Quarter Financial Results and Significant Operational Trends and Events

evidence.text: Operating margin decreased to 10.6%, as compared to 11.4%; Operating margin decreased to 8.8%, as compared to 9.0%; Operating margin increased to 8.1%, as compared to 2.8%
evidence.subsection: Three months ended June 30, 2022 compared to the three months ended June 30, 2021

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Mixed signals with expanding gross margins but compressing operating margins.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include supply chain disruptions, delays, weather impacts, and economic uncertainties. These risks have already impacted operational efficiency and financial results, with supply chain issues and weather-related events being particularly significant.

evidence.text: The decrease in operating margin was primarily due to the normal variability associated with the overall timing of projects and project mix, as well as inefficiencies attributable to supply chain disruptions impacting certain operations and elevated consumables costs;
evidence.subsection: Current Quarter Financial Results and Significant Operational Trends and Events

evidence.text: The decrease in operating margin was attributable to significant additional costs arising from delays on a large transmission project in Canada that were due to the continued negative impact of the COVID-19 pandemic from the first quarter;
evidence.subsection: Significant Factors Impacting Results

evidence.text: The results of our business in a given period can be impacted by adverse weather conditions, severe weather events, natural disasters or other emergencies, which include, among other things, heavy or prolonged snowfall or rainfall, hurricanes, tropical storms, tornadoes, floods, blizzards, extreme temperatures, wildfires, post-wildfire floods and debris flows, pandemics (including the ongoing COVID-19 pandemic), and earthquakes.
evidence.subsection: Significant Factors Impacting Results

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Near-term operational and financial impacts from various risks.
